owner.bio: I build small, calm software and let it grow slowly — mostly interfaces, occasionally little worlds. Every planet here is a project I've cared about.

planets.name: Aster
planets.desc: A tiny to-do app where finishing a task grows a little plant. It's my longest-running side project and, somehow, my calmest one.

planets.name: Comet CLI
planets.desc: A command-line companion that fetches, formats and ships things before your coffee gets cold. No screenshots here — it's a terminal, and proud of it.

planets.name: Halcyon
planets.desc: A focus timer wrapped in generative ambient sound. Built during one very loud month in one very small apartment.

planets.name: Terra
planets.tagline: maps for places that don't exist
planets.desc: A little editor for mapping places that don't exist — coastlines, biomes, and creatures that wander when you're not looking.

planets.name: Bloom
planets.desc: A mood journal that grows a different flower for every entry. Private by default, gentle on purpose.

planets.name: Quasar
planets.desc: Realtime dashboards designed to be glanced at, not stared at. Calm colors, honest numbers, zero blinking red.

planets.name: Pebble
planets.desc: A featherweight micro-blog engine. You write a sentence, it becomes a page. That's the entire pitch.

planets.name: Orbit
planets.desc: The open-source scheduler I always wished existed — timezone-sane, meeting-light, and deeply anti-3pm-meeting.
